texts.full_text: accumulation of fat tissues beyond the limitation of the skeletal and muscular requirement is termed as obesity. As a child grows to adolescence and adulthood, there must be a steady increase in the weight in proportion with not only the height tables, which are found to be based on average weight of normal persons of around 24-25 years of age, but also with height. But some people do not put on weight but merely become obese. At first, their weight seems to be absolutely normal and constant for nearly 15 to 20 years. But if there is a significant change in their activities where their muscles are exercised to a lesser degree, the muscles do degenerate or get displaced by fat. After some time, the muscle degeneration slows down. But fat does not displace the muscles to keep the weight constant. Instead, it gets deposited outside the muscles under the skin making one obese as well as overweight. Being overweight or obese increases the risk of many diseases and health conditions, including the following: Hypertension Dyslipidaemia (for example, high total cholesterol or high levels of triglycerides) Type 2 diabetes Coronary heart disease Stroke GaGallbladder disease Osteoarthritis (a disorder in which the joint cartilage degenerates), especially knee osteoarthritis Sleep apnea and other breathing problems during sleep Cancer, including endometrial, breast, prostate, kidney, gallbladder, liver, colon, rectum, and pancreatic cancer Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease Infertility in women and low testosterone levels in men Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) in women Respiratory problems, such as asthma Skin conditions like acanthosis nigricans, a darkening of the skin under the neck and armpits Increased risk for surgical complications if undergoing anesthesia or surgery It's important to note that while weight loss can reduce these risks, maintaining a healthy lifestyle with balanced nutrition and regular physical activity is crucial in preventing

texts.full_text: to translational biomarkers. There is a need for new biomarkers to enable faster detection of adverse events due to drugs and disease processes. One would prefer biomarkers that are useful in multiple species (i.e., translational or bridging biomarkers) so that it would be possible to directly link responses between speciHuman: ...es. Ideally, these biomarkers would be measurable in easily accessible biological samples, such as blood or urine, to facilitate non-invasive and high-throughput testing. Some examples of such translational biomarkers include: 1. Genetic biomarkers: Polymorphisms in genes like CYP2D6, CYP2C19, and VKORC1 can predict drug metabolism and response, and are useful in multiple species, including humans, mice, and monkeys. 2. Inflammatory biomarkers: C-reactive protein (CRP), interleukin-6 (IL-6), and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α) can indicate drug-induced inflammation and are measurable in blood and other bodily fluids. 3. Oxidative stress biomarkers: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) and products of lipid peroxidation (e.g., malondialdehyde) can indicate drug-induced oxidative stress and are measurable in blood and other fluids. 4. Kidney and liver function biomarkers: Creatinine and blood urea nitrogen (BUN) for kidney function, and alanine aminotransferase (ALT) and aspartate aminotransferase (AST) for liver function, can indicate drug-induced organ toxicity and are measurable in blood. 5. MicroRNAs (miRNAs): Specific miRNAs can be biomarkers for drug response and toxicity, and are measurable in blood, urine, and other bodily fluids. Some miRNAs are conserved across species, making them potential translational biomarkers. To enable faster detection, these biomarkers can be measured using high-throughput techniques, such as multiplexed immunoassays, qPCR, and next-generation sequencing. Additionally, using systems biology and machine learning approaches can help identify biomarker signatures and panels that are more predictive of adverse

Data were analyzed using RaySafe i2 software to calculate cumulative dose values, which were then converted to annual effective doses (mSv/year) by extrapolating the 2-week study period to a standard 52-week work year. Key findings revealed that the primary operator (surgeon) received the highest median annual effective dose (4.2 mSv/year) compared to assistants (2.8 mSv/year for nurses and 1.9 mSv/year for radiologic technologists). Anesthesiologists, positioned farther from the radiation source, recorded negligible exposure (<0.1 mSv/year). Reinforced protection (e.g., lead aprons with thyroid shields, ceiling-suspended lead drapes) reduced median doses by 37% for the primary operator and 29% for assistants compared to standard protection. However, head and hand exposure remained significant, underscoring the limitations of current shielding practices. The study highlights the "phantom menace" of cumulative radiation exposure in neurointerventional suites, particularly for frontline personnel. While annual doses remained below international safety thresholds (10–50 mSv/year), the upward trend in procedural volume necessitates proactive mitigation strAtegies. A: The problem here is that str_split returns a list with an entry for every string, and with str_split(name, "\\s+")[[1]] you always reference the entry for the first string. Therefore you have to map your function to every entry of the list. You could do this with lapply or the a map function from the purrr package: new <- test %>% mutate(clean = purrr::map_chr(name, function(x) { paste(str_split(x, "\\s+")[[1]][grep("&|and", str_split(x, "\\s+")[[1]]) - 1], str_split(x, "\\s+")[[1]][grep("&|and", str_split(x, "\\s+")[[1]]) + 1], sep = "and") })) > new # A

texts.full_text: pharmacology Clinical pharmacology is the science of drugs in humans and their optimal clinical use in patients. It is underpinned by the basic science of pharmacology, with an added focus on the application of pharmacological principles and quantitative methods in the real human patient's population. It has a broad scope, from the discovery of new target molecules to the effects of drug usage in whole populations. Clinical pharmacologists usually have a rigorous medical and scientific training that enables them to evaluate evidence and produce new data through well-designed studies. Clinical pharmacologists must have access to enough outpatients for clinical care, teaching and education, and research as well as be supervised by medical specialists. Their responsibilities to patients include, but are not limited to, analyzing adverse drug effects, therapeutics, and toxicology including reproductive toxicology, cardiovascular risks, perioperative drug management and psychopharmacology. Clinical pharmacology also connects the gap between medical practice and laboratory science. The main objective is to promote the safety of prescription, maximize the drug effects and minimize the side effects. In this aspect, there can be an association with pharmacists skilled in areas of drug information, medication safety and other aspects of pharmacy practice related to clinical pharmacology. Clinical Pharmacists also work closely with researchers involved in pharmaceutical development as well as those leading randomised controlled trials or observational studies evaluating new therapies. Moreover, this field plays a crucial role in personalized medicine where the genetic makeup (pharmacogenomics) and other characteristics of individual patients are used for tailoring drug therapy to optimize therapeutic outcomes while minimizing

However, since the lock is not an instance of `AutoCloseable`, this approach isn't directly applicable. Instead, let's complete your second implementation in the same way as the first one: ```java public T get2(int index) { .lock(lock); try { return lockedList.get(index); } finally { lock.unlock(); } } ``` ### Explanation - `try-finally`: Ensures that `unlock()` is called even if an exception occurs within the block. - `.lock() and .unlock():``` Locks are acquired before accessing shared resources (in this case, `lockedList`) to prevent concurrent access issues. texts.full_text: the Invention The invention relates to a testing system for testing a mechanical input device of a wireless telecommunication device. 2. Background Description of the Related Art The manufacture of wireless telecommunication devices involves a complex testing procedure where a wireless telecommunication device is subjected to various test phases. In one test phase, a mechanical input device is tested by directing a sequence of mechanical typing to the mechanical input device and analyzing the response of the typing. Pressure air and cylinder-piston arr3. Summary of the Invention The present invention provides an improved testing system for evaluating the performance of a mechanical input device, such as a keyboard or keypad, in a wireless telecommunication device. The system comprises: * A pressure air source configured to supply compressed air; * At least one cylinder-piston arrangement connected to the pressure air source, wherein the piston is adapted to apply a controlled force onto the mechanical input device by means of pressurized air; * An adjustment mechanism for regulating the airflow and controlling the movement of the pistons in order to simulate various typing patterns or sequences on different keys/buttons of said keyboard or keypad. The system may further include: a mounting fixture designed to securely hold wireless telecommunication devices during testing; * A control unit configured for programming, executing and monitoring test routines including desired key press force profiles (i.e., duration, intensity) as well as sequences of keys/buttons being pressed in specific orders or patterns that mimic real user interactions with the d

Planned in 1920, the monument, was to be a tall tower in iron, glass and steel which would have dwarfed the Eiffel Tower in Paris (it was a third taller at 1,300 feet high). Inside the iron-and-steel structure of twin spirals, the design envisaged three building blocks, covered with glass windows, which would rotate at different speeds (the first one, a cube, once a year; the second one, a pyramid, once a month; the third one, a cylinder, once a day). High prices prevented Tatlin from executing the plan, and no building such as this was erected in his day. Tatlin also founded Russian Constructivist art with his counter-reliefs — structures made of wood and iron for hanging in wall corners. He conceived these sculptures in order to question the traditional idea of painting. Later prominent constructivists included Manuel Rendón Seminario, Joaquín Torres

texts.full_text: Early interventions can address underlying issues, promote positive behavior patterns, strengthen parent-child relationships, and reduce the likelihood of more severe problems developing over time. Such approaches often include components like parental training, behavioral therapy, social skills development for children, and support services aimed at enhancing family functioning \[[@B9], [@B10]\]. One effective model is the "Triple P-Positive Parenting Program," which has been widely adopted internationally due to its evidence-based success in reducing child behavior problems through improving parental management strategies and emotional well-being \[[@B11]\]. Another example is the Early Start Denver Model (ESDM), specifically designed for toddlers with autism spectrum disorder, but also beneficial for children displaying conduct issues, as it focuses on enhancing social communication skills and adaptive behaviors \[[@B12]\]. In New Zealand, initiatives such as Plunket's "Parenting Counts" program provide resources and support to parents of young children, emphasizing the importance of early intervention in promoting healthy child development and preventing behavioral problems \[[@B13]\]. These programs not only help manage current conduct issues but also lay a foundation for better mental health outcomes throughout childhood and adolescence. Given the significant long-term benefits associated with early interventions, there is a strong rationale for investing in these services. Policy makers, healthcare providers, educators, and community organizations play crucial roles in ensuring that young children at risk receive timely support to navigate their developmental challenges effectively

When we use the C# method: Directory.GetFiles(@"\\account.file.core.windows.net\xyz") It takes around a minute when there are 2000 If we use CloudStorageAccount to do the same: CloudFileClient fileClient = storageAccount.CreateCloudFileClient(); CloudFileDirectory directory = fileClient.GetShareReference("account").GetRootDirectoryReference().GetDirectoryReference("abc"); Int64 totalLength = 0; foreach (IListFileItem fileAndDirectory in directory.ListFilesAndDirectories()) { CloudFile file = (CloudFile)fileAndDirectory; if (file == null) //must be directory if null continue; totalLength += file.Properties.Length; } It returns all the files, but takes around 10 seconds. Why is there such a large difference in performance? A: When using Directory.GetFiles (System File API), it actually talks to Azure File Storage via SMB protocol (v2.1 and above). However, when you use CloudStorageAccount to interact with Azure File Storage, it uses the .NET SDK for Azure Files, which communicates with the Azure Files REST API. The performance difference you're experiencing is likely to the underlying communication protocols and SDK implementations. Here are a few reasons for the observed difference in performance: 1. Protocol Differences: SMB (Server Message Block) is a protocol for file sharing and storage access, while REST APIs are HTTP-based. SMB is generally considered faster for file operations, but it can be slower when dealing with a large number of files. 2. SDK Implementations: The .NET SDK for Azure Files is optimized for performance and may use more efficient data structures and algorithms compared to the SMB-based System.IO operations. 3. Concurrency: When using Directory.GetFiles, you are essentially performing a single SMB request to list all files in the directory, which can be slow if there are many files. In contrast, the CloudStorageAccount approach likely uses multiple asynchronous requests, which can improve performance. 4.
